name: Discord
description: Integrates with Discord REST API v10 for bot messaging, channel management, and server communication
setup_instructions:
## Get a Discord bot token

Discord uses a **bot token** (not a personal account password). You create the bot once in the Discord Developer Portal, then paste its token here.

### 1. Create the Discord application + bot
1. Open the **Discord Developer Portal**: https://discord.com/developers/applications
2. Log in with your Discord account.
3. Click **New Application** (top right). Name it something like `T1 Agentics Bot` and accept the developer terms.
4. In the left sidebar of your new app, click **Bot**.
5. Click **Reset Token** (or **Copy** if a token is already shown). Discord only shows the full token once — copy it immediately.

### 2. Grant the bot the permissions it needs
In the **Bot** page, under **Privileged Gateway Intents**, you do NOT need any of these enabled for the actions T1 supports.

In the **OAuth2 → URL Generator** page (left sidebar):
  - Scopes: check **`bot`**
  - Bot Permissions: at minimum check **`Send Messages`**, **`Read Message History`**, **`View Channels`**. If you plan to use `create_channel`, also check **`Manage Channels`**.
  - Copy the **Generated URL** at the bottom and open it in a browser. Pick the Discord server ("guild") to add the bot to and confirm.

### 3. Get the channel IDs you'll send to
1. In your Discord client: **User Settings → Advanced → Developer Mode → On**.
2. Right-click any channel and choose **Copy Channel ID** — that's the value you'll pass to the `send_message` action's `channel_id` parameter.
3. Right-click the server name → **Copy Server ID** for the `guild_id` parameter.

### 4. Paste the bot token below
Paste the **raw token** in the **Token** field. **Do not include the `Bot ` prefix** — T1 adds that automatically.

Then click **Test Authentication** to confirm.

### Troubleshooting
- **`401 Unauthorized`**: Token is wrong or has been reset. Reset it in the Developer Portal and try again.
- **`403 Missing Access`**: The bot isn't in the server. Re-run the OAuth2 invite URL.
- **`403 Missing Permissions`** on `send_message`: The bot can see the channel but can't post. Edit the channel → Permissions → add the bot with **Send Messages**.

actions.name: Send Message
actions.description: Send a message to a Discord channel

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: Channel ID to send the message to

parameters.name: embeds
parameters.description: JSON array of embed objects for rich content

parameters.name: message_reference
parameters.description: JSON object for replying to a message

actions.name: List Guild Channels
actions.description: List all channels in a Discord guild/server

actions.name: Get Messages
actions.description: Retrieve messages from a Discord channel

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Get messages before this message ID

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Get messages after this message ID

parameters.name: around
parameters.description: Get messages around this message ID

actions.name: Create Guild Channel
actions.description: Create a new channel in a Discord guild/server

parameters.name: topic
parameters.description: Channel topic (max 1024 chars for text, 4096 for forum)

parameters.name: embeds
parameters.description: Updated JSON array of embed objects

actions.name: Get Channel
actions.description: Get details of a specific Discord channel
